stories.headline: Tyreek Hill's Busted Wheel Won't Mend Till Midseason, and He's Ridin' Free
stories.read: Grim smoke off the Miami wire, pardner: word from Schefter is Tyreek Hill's busted knee might not mend till the season's halfway gone, and the Dolphins cut him loose to wander the open range as a free man. That's a dry well at the draft table, friend, so don't go payin' top dollar for a horse that won't run till November. The real gold's in who he left behind: Jaylen Waddle saddles back up as the lead pass-catcher, and cheap hands like Malik Washington and Nick Westbrook-Ikhine just inherited a fatter cut of the throws. Go pan those creeks while the saloon's still starin' at Hill's empty stall.

stories.headline: Rashee Rice: Top-Shelf Gold Sittin' Under a Thundercloud
stories.read: Here's a vein that gushes gold but sits right under a storm, pardner. The Chiefs ain't in any hurry to re-sign Rashee Rice, and a probation stumble's got the whole territory frettin' the sheriff could bench him a spell. But the surgeon cleared his knee, he's set to break camp on schedule, and in a thin Kansas City receivin' corps he's fixin' to lead the outfit in throws. That's top-five upside at a spooked-down price, so if you've got the bench to weather a squall, this here's your high-risk, high-reward strike.

stories.headline: Pickens Saddles Up on the Tag, No Holdout in Dallas
stories.read: Good news rides in from the Dallas ranch, pardner: George Pickens quit dickerin' and will play this year on his twenty-seven-million-dollar tag, and he swears there's no holdout comin', he'll report to camp right on schedule. For your roster that spells peace of mind, no summer standoff to fret over all August. He's the clear second gun opposite CeeDee Lamb, and last fall he ran with the big herd for a whole mess of points. Priced 'round the tenth receiver off the board, that's a fair claim with no drama, so ride him and don't overthink it.
